items.title: Richard Scolyer, who challenged the world to 'be brave, be bold', dies aged 59

children.content: The 2024 Australian of the Year, Richard Scolyer, has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

articles.title: Former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer's 'final farewell'

children.content: Professor Scolyer wrote he "would be delighted and humbled to be remembered as a proud everyday Aussie who 'gave it a crack', and in doing so, inspired others to pursue their dreams and passions with humility, love and compassion".

image.altText: A man in a suit a tie smiles, in front of Australian and Aboriginal flags.

gallery.altText: Professor Scolyer sits leaning on his elbow in an outdoor seat. He wears a blue-collared shirt and jeans and is smiling

thumbnail.altText: Professor Scolyer sits leaning on his elbow in an outdoor seat. He wears a blue-collared shirt and jeans and is smiling

items.title: Iran fires missiles at Israel's north as a 'warning' after strikes in Beirut

children.content: The strikes from Iran are the first such bombardment since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April. Follow live.

image.altText: A flash of light above a city skyline at night.

items.title: Iran briefing: Day 100 of this war ends in a way we all saw coming

children.content: This was day 100 of this war, and it's left us with a tinderbox which could be about to explode, writes our correspondent Matthew Doran.

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

gallery.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

thumbnail.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A close-up shows a man's face next to the label on the outside of a metal chemical drum

items.title: Tasmania's population boom has gone bust — and it spells trouble

children.content: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt — and big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

image.altText: Family of three pose for photo on suburban street with their bikes

items.title: 'Duck that fight': Husic accuses government of cowering to Trump on AI

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could cause more harm.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and business shirt sitting in a chair during an interview with a neutral expression on his face.

items.title: Anthropic wants caution but the market wants more AI

children.content: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

items.title: From migration to AUKUS, more politicians are speaking out

children.content: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

image.altText: Man standing in a suit in the senate

items.title: The Top End town where 15 people died preventable deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

image.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car

items.title: Maths genius, devoted mum, headline-making CEO on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

gallery.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?

children.content: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

image.altText: A woman wearing dark rimmed glasses, a white hard hat and orange high vis vest stands in front of similarly dressed people.

items.title: Quick hits: Monaco GP interrupted for safety as Kardashians keep up with F1

children.content: From a dodgy section of track to a mega celebrity watching her partner equal a record with the great Ayrton Senna, the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix had plenty of talking points.

image.altText: Kim Kardashian looks on in parc ferme at Monaco

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

gallery.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Shrek, Captain Cook and the stars: Welcome to Brian Robinson's multiverse

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

image.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a long grey beard and an earring in his right ear, wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Six filmmakers had 10 days to make a movie overseas. See how they went

children.content: From illegal fishing in the Philippines to Michael Jackson impersonators in Las Vegas, catch up on the films from the very first week of Race Around The World.

image.altText: A fisherman sits on the end of his boat

items.title: Why sentiment in Japan has turned against foreigners despite a worker shortage

children.content: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrocketed in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

gallery.altText: Sohei Kamiya, holding two microphones tape together, speaking with a closed fist in the air.

thumbnail.altText: Sohei Kamiya, holding two microphones tape together, speaking with a closed fist in the air.

items.title: Qld government contemplates ditching anti-junk-mail measures

children.content: A new consultation paper argues the measures are too burdensome and recommends repealing state laws and making local councils responsible for combating junk mail.

image.altText: A grid of apartment letterboxes with 'no junk mail' stickers on each.

items.title: Zverev claims first major with French Open victory

children.content: In his fourth appearance in the final of a major tournament, Alexander Zverev lifts the French Open trophy following a five-set grind against Flavio Cobolli.

image.altText: Alexander Zverev of Germany kisses the Coupe des Mousquetaires trophy at the French Open.

items.title: Victorian teachers sue over alleged unpaid superannuation

children.content: A class action alleges the Education Department short-changed teachers some $100 million in superannuation contributions between 1995 and 2023.

gallery.altText: A woman with chin length wavy blonde hair with a two-toned orange scarf around her neck sits with patchworking on her lap.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with chin length wavy blonde hair with a two-toned orange scarf around her neck sits with patchworking on her lap.

avatar.alt: Sacha Payne, dressed in a red jacket and white shirt, stands outside Victorian Parliament.

items.title: Disability community says NDIS 'fighting the wrong battles' over funding plans

children.content: The agency spent $60 million on lawyers to fight participants at a tribunal last financial year. Disability advocates say there should be greater focus on the NDIS's legal fees as cuts to the scheme are being made.

image.altText: A man and a woman both in wheelchair going down a walkway with trees

items.title: Outback cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'

children.content: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

image.altText: Mount Isa cake decorator Ishani Harney holds a shard of glass from her shopfront window that was broken during a 2023 break-in.

items.title: Players left in tears as Danish star Eriksen collapses during friendly

children.content: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

image.altText: Danish players show their distress after Christian Eriksen's collapse.

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: As rape allegations engulf MAFS UK, Channel 4's former boss says the risks were always there

children.content: Married at First Sight UK could be axed as its broadcaster, Channel 4, awaits an external review into the program and serious rape allegations before "deciding what happens next".

gallery.altText: A building with people walking past outside. There is a large '4' sign outside the building.

thumbnail.altText: A building with people walking past outside. There is a large '4' sign outside the building.

items.title: New mission for volunteers who helped find Celine and Peter

children.content: A group of volunteers helped find the remains of two long-term missing people in Tasmania this year. Now, they're planning to look for Michael Vrankovic, who has not been seen since January.

image.altText: A group of people wearing high visibility clothing search in a line, walking through dry eucalypt forest in the mist.

items.title: Hundreds in Nigeria freed from militant group Boko Haram

children.content: Hundreds of men, women and children abducted by militant group Boko Haram have been rescued, according to the Nigerian military.

image.altText: Men, woman and children gather on the ground at night under flood lights

items.title: Famous outback race claims another life after serious motorbike crash

children.content: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

image.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

items.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in WA shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

image.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station on a gopher, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

gallery.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

thumbnail.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

items.title: Blues beat Dons to make it four in a row, Swans pip Saints in a thriller

children.content: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

image.altText: An AFL player in dark blue celebrates a goal, framed by players in red and black

items.title: Aussies crowned rugby sevens world champions after conquering NZ

children.content: Maddison Levi ignores a knee injury to score a superb double as Australia wraps up the women's rugby sevens World Championship in France.

image.altText: Australia celebrates winning Bordeaux leg of rugby sevens World Championship.

items.title: How Beethoven's Ninth defined a genre, and the composers who got left out

children.content: Beethoven's Ninth has topped the ABC Classic 100 countdown for the fifth time. We explore its mystique and shed light on composers following in Beethoven's footsteps.

children.content: Thousands of Australians voted in this year's ABC Classic 100. Here's what the numbers told us about your listening habits.

children.content: After tens of thousands of votes in the 2026 ABC Classic 100 Ludwig van Beethoven has been crowned Greatest Of All Time. Look back at the full countdown.
